steps.title: Identify what type of evidence works
steps.content: The key signal is the second sentence: “Only material objects—such as...fossil remains—can show whether a particular idea...is defensible.” The passage specifies that paleontologists need evidence grounded in tangible, observable materials.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content: Physical exactly fits: it describes evidence from material objects you can examine and test—what the passage requires. Convenient and creative don't name types of evidence; inconclusive contradicts the passage's claim that evidence must show whether ideas are defensible. That matches choice A — A is correct.
